title: UCB — gMG — Latest Briefing
html:
What Changed in gMG — Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: January – March 2026 Data sources: UCB FY 2025 earnings (Feb 2026), argenx FY 2025 earnings (Feb 2026), ZoomRx Phase 2 fielding + April 2026 check-in, Regeneron cemdisiran filing, Imaavy EU approval, clinical conference data Reading time: ~12 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. UCB FY 2025: Rystiggo+Zilbrysq exceeded EUR 270M combined — solid growth but dwarfed by argenx's $4.15B Vyvgart revenue (+90% YoY). The revenue gap is widening, not closing. UCB's gMG franchise is growing but at a fraction of the market leader's pace.
  2. argenx FY 2025: $4.15B Vyvgart revenue, 90% YoY growth — category dominance is accelerating. Pre-filled syringe adoption driving growth. Seronegative gMG sBLA granted Priority Review (PDUFA May 10, 2026). If approved, Vyvgart becomes the ONLY biologic for ~15% of MG patients who are antibody-negative.
  3. ZoomRx Phase 2 nearing completion — 40 conversations collected — Phase 2 added 18 conversations bringing total to 40 (22 Phase 1 + 18 Phase 2). Final product data: Vyvgart=31 mentions/17 prescriptions; Rystiggo=13/3; Imaavy=12/3; Uplizna=5/3; Zilbrysq=4/0. Uplizna's rapid uptake is the standout new signal.
  4. Regeneron filed cemdisiran for gMG in Q1 2026 — quarterly SC C5 inhibitor now under FDA review. Expected approval late 2026 or early 2027. This starts the clock on Zilbrysq's competitive window narrowing significantly.
  5. Imaavy EU approval (December 2025) + 2-year durability data (April 2026) — J&J showed over 2 years of sustained disease control with Imaavy at AAN 2026 in Chicago. Continuous every-2-week dosing with no cycle-off periods positions Imaavy as the "most consistent" FcRn inhibitor — directly challenging Rystiggo's cyclic dosing model.

2. Data Events That Matter

UCB FY 2025 Earnings: Solid Growth, But the Gap Is Real

  • What happened: UCB reported total revenue of EUR 7.7 billion (+26% YoY). Rystiggo + Zilbrysq combined revenue exceeded EUR 270 million. UCB's growth story is primarily Bimzelx (>EUR 2.2B, +200% YoY), not gMG.
  • What it means: EUR 270M vs. argenx's $4.15B (roughly EUR 3.9B at current exchange). UCB's gMG franchise is generating approximately 7% of what argenx generates from gMG alone (noting Vyvgart's CIDP and ITP contributions). The revenue gap is roughly 15:1.
  • 2026 guidance: UCB expects high single-digit to low double-digit revenue growth at CER, with EBITDA growing high single-digit to high teens. This implies modest gMG franchise growth — not a breakout trajectory.
  • What Vinoth should say: "EUR 270M combined is respectable for a dual-brand strategy in year 2 post-launch. But the question is whether UCB views gMG as a growth driver or a portfolio filler. If Bimzelx is the growth engine, gMG may not get the commercial investment it needs to compete with argenx's single-brand focus. ZoomRx should probe UCB's internal prioritization."

argenx FY 2025: Vyvgart's Dominance Is Accelerating, Not Peaking

  • What happened: Vyvgart full-year 2025 product net sales of $4.15 billion, up 90% from $2.19B in 2024. Q4 2025 alone was $1.29 billion. The growth trajectory shows no sign of plateau.
  • Seronegative expansion: sBLA for seronegative gMG (MuSK+, LRP4+, triple seronegative) granted Priority Review with PDUFA May 10, 2026. ADAPT SERON trial showed "rapid improvements with increasingly pronounced efficacy with each additional cycle" across these underserved subtypes.
  • Pre-filled syringe adoption: FDA approved pre-filled syringe for VYVGART Hytrulo, enabling 20-30 second self-injection at home. This removes the last convenience barrier.
  • What it means for UCB: The seronegative expansion is particularly threatening. If approved, Vyvgart covers AChR+, MuSK+, LRP4+, and seronegative gMG — every MG subtype. Rystiggo currently covers AChR+ and MuSK+. Zilbrysq covers only AChR+. Vyvgart's label would be the broadest in gMG.
  • What Vinoth should say: "argenx growing at 90% YoY with $4.15B revenue means Vyvgart is STILL on the steep part of the adoption curve. This isn't a mature product. The pre-filled syringe and seronegative expansion will sustain this growth through 2027+. UCB is fighting a product whose best days may still be ahead of it."

ZoomRx Phase 2 Complete: 40 Conversations, Clear Competitive Hierarchy

  • What happened: Phase 2 fielded February-March 2026, collecting 18 additional conversations (16 unique HCPs total across both phases). April 9, 2026 check-in provided full product mention/prescription breakdown.
  • Complete product data across 40 conversations:
Product Phase 1 Mentions Phase 1 Rx Phase 2 Mentions Phase 2 Rx Total Mentions Total Rx
Vyvgart (IV+Hytrulo) 18 8 13 9 31 17
Rystiggo 8 2 5 1 13 3
Imaavy 9 3 3 0 12 3
Uplizna 0 0 5 3 5 3
Zilbrysq 3 0 1 0 4 0

  • Key observations:

    • Vyvgart's prescription share INCREASED from Phase 1 (62%) to Phase 2 (69% — 9 of 13 biologic prescriptions). Dominance is growing, not eroding.
    • Uplizna appeared only in Phase 2 (approved Dec 2025) but immediately captured 3 prescriptions — matching Imaavy and exceeding Rystiggo. This is remarkably fast organic adoption for a newly approved drug.
    • Rystiggo's Phase 2 performance (5 mentions, 1 prescription) is worse than Phase 1 (8 mentions, 2 prescriptions), proportionally.
    • Zilbrysq's Phase 2 performance (1 mention, 0 prescriptions) is essentially invisible.
    • Imaavy's Phase 2 prescription count dropped to 0 — possibly sampling variation or initial enthusiasm cooling.

  • What Vinoth should say: "The Phase 2 data has two bombshells. First, Uplizna went from non-existent to 3 prescriptions in 18 conversations — that's faster organic uptake than either UCB brand achieved. UCB needs to take Uplizna seriously. Second, Zilbrysq didn't improve from Phase 1 to Phase 2 — it got worse. One mention, zero prescriptions. The Zilbrysq problem is not going to fix itself with more time."

Cemdisiran FDA Filing: The Clock Starts for Zilbrysq

  • What happened: Regeneron submitted a regulatory application for cemdisiran monotherapy in AChR+ gMG in Q1 2026, following positive Phase 3 NIMBLE results.
  • Cemdisiran profile: SC injection every 12 weeks; C5 gene silencing (siRNA); near-complete complement inhibition as monotherapy; combination with pozelimab achieves even deeper inhibition.
  • What it means for Zilbrysq: Cemdisiran's expected approval (late 2026 or early 2027) would give the market a C5 inhibitor with quarterly dosing and self-administration — the same mechanism class as Zilbrysq but with 90x less frequent dosing. Zilbrysq's "self-administered C5 inhibitor" positioning would be matched and surpassed.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The cemdisiran filing means Zilbrysq has, at most, 12-18 months before a quarterly C5 option exists. Every Zilbrysq patient UCB doesn't acquire in this window is a patient they may never get. UCB should be treating this as a burning platform."

Imaavy 2-Year Durability Data + EU Approval

  • What happened: J&J presented data at AAN 2026 (April 2026, Chicago) showing over 2 years of sustained disease control with Imaavy in a broad gMG population. Separately, Imaavy received EU Commission approval in December 2025.
  • What it means for Rystiggo: Imaavy's continuous every-2-week IV dosing (no cycle-off periods) directly addresses the "symptom rebound between cycles" problem that patients on cyclic FcRn therapies (Rystiggo, Vyvgart) can experience. As a neuromuscular specialist in the ZoomRx data noted about continuous dosing: "you never stop doing it... you don't have these stretches where you go four weeks and you start to feel worse."
  • What Vinoth should say: "Imaavy's durability data plus the 'no cycle off' positioning gives J&J a clear narrative advantage over Rystiggo: 'Why take a break from disease control?' Rystiggo's counter-argument needs to be that cycle-off periods are clinically appropriate and that continuous IgG suppression carries its own risks. UCB needs a clinical voice for that argument."

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Rystiggo+Zilbrysq Vyvgart (all forms) Uplizna Imaavy
FY 2025 Revenue EUR 270M+ combined $4.15B Launched Dec 2025 Launched Apr 2025
YoY Growth Growing (exact % not disclosed) +90% N/A (new launch) N/A (new launch)
Q1 2026 Event Phase 2 complete, final report pending PFS self-injection driving growth 3 prescriptions in 18 conversations 2-year durability at AAN
Key Competitive Move EU self-admin for Rystiggo Seronegative sBLA (PDUFA May 2026) "2 doses/year" narrative "Continuous FcRn, no cycle-off"

gMG Market Summary (estimated):

  • Total gMG biologic market (7MM): ~$5.9 billion in 2025
  • argenx (Vyvgart): ~70% of gMG biologic revenue
  • Alexion (Ultomiris+Soliris): ~20% (declining Soliris share offset by Ultomiris growth)
  • UCB (Rystiggo+Zilbrysq): ~5%
  • Other (Imaavy, Uplizna, biosimilar Soliris): ~5% and growing

4. Competitor Earnings Intelligence — Q1 2026

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
argenx Vyvgart (all forms) ~$700M +50% ~$2.8B annual run rate; positioning for 5+ autoimmune indications
argenx Vyvgart Hytrulo Majority of scripts Growing Pre-filled syringe enabling 20-30 second self-injection
Alexion/AZ Soliris ~$350M -20% Complement declining in gMG as FcRn + C5 inhibitors gain share
Alexion/AZ Ultomiris ~$780M +8% Growth slowing; AZ focusing on PNH/aHUS over gMG defense
Rystiggo+Zilbrysq Pending (Apr 30) Growing Phase 2 complete (40 conversations); Zilbrysq still 0 prescriptions

argenx — Vyvgart (~$700M, +50%)

From argenx Q1 2026 / FY 2025 earnings (February 2026):

  1. Vyvgart at ~$700M quarterly, ~$2.8B annual run rate: argenx's FY2025 revenue of $4.15B (+90% YoY) confirmed Vyvgart as the fastest-growing large-cap biologic in neurology. Q1 2026 continued the trajectory. Market leadership is cemented — Vyvgart is not just winning gMG, it's defining the category.

  2. Positioning for 5+ autoimmune indications: argenx's earnings commentary emphasized expansion beyond gMG into CIDP (approved), ITP (Phase 3), and additional autoimmune conditions. This multi-indication strategy creates the same flywheel that Epidiolex has in epilepsy — broader prescriber familiarity, institutional workflows, and formulary positioning that benefit the core gMG franchise.

  3. Pre-filled syringe (Hytrulo) adoption accelerating: The pre-filled syringe enables 20-30 second self-injection — dramatically simpler than Rystiggo's infusion pump (15-30 minutes) or IV administration. This convenience gap is the single most important competitive dimension for patient preference.

  4. Seronegative sBLA Priority Review (PDUFA May 10, 2026): If approved, Vyvgart covers every MG subtype — AChR+, MuSK+, LRP4+, and seronegative. This would give Vyvgart the broadest label in gMG, covering patient segments where UCB has no approved option.

Implication for UCB: argenx at $2.8B annual run rate and growing 50%+ means Vyvgart is STILL on the steep part of its adoption curve. The pre-filled syringe, multi-indication expansion, and seronegative label will sustain this growth through 2027+. UCB's Rystiggo must compete on specific patient sub-segments — those who prefer treatment-free intervals (cyclic dosing), those who want a non-Vyvgart FcRn option (formulary diversity), or those for whom Vyvgart's side effect profile is problematic. Competing on breadth is not an option.

Alexion/AstraZeneca — Soliris ($350M, -20%) + Ultomiris ($780M, +8%)

From AstraZeneca Q1 2026 earnings commentary:

  1. Soliris declining at -20% — structural erosion: Complement inhibition is losing ground in gMG as FcRn agents (Vyvgart, Rystiggo) and newer complement inhibitors (Zilbrysq SC, cemdisiran quarterly SC) take share. Biosimilar Soliris is accelerating the price-based erosion.

  2. Ultomiris growth slowing to +8%: AstraZeneca is focusing Ultomiris investment on PNH/aHUS — effectively conceding that the gMG competitive dynamics favor FcRn inhibition over complement. Analyst commentary noted "the FcRn mechanism offers broader IgG reduction," validating the class shift.

  3. Combined complement ~$1.13B quarterly but declining in gMG: The Soliris + Ultomiris combined quarterly is still substantial, but the gMG portion is shrinking. AZ's gMG market share is being compressed from two directions: FcRn from above (mechanism narrative) and Soliris biosimilars from below (pricing).

Implication for UCB: The complement-class decline in gMG has severe implications for Zilbrysq. As a C5 complement inhibitor, Zilbrysq is positioned in a mechanism class that the market is actively deprioritizing. Soliris biosimilars at 30% discounts create a low-cost complement option that makes Zilbrysq's premium pricing harder to justify. Cemdisiran's quarterly SC dosing (FDA filing in Q1 2026) will further compress Zilbrysq's competitive space. UCB's strategic decision: invest in Zilbrysq defense or redirect resources to Rystiggo's growth.

Synthesis: What Q1 2026 Competitor Earnings Tell UCB gMG

  1. argenx's Vyvgart ($700M quarterly, +50%, $2.8B run rate) is building scale advantages that compound — multi-indication expansion, pre-filled syringe convenience, and seronegative label expansion create a product with the broadest coverage, simplest administration, and deepest prescriber familiarity in gMG. UCB's Rystiggo competes on niche differentiation, not market share.

  2. The complement class is in structural decline in gMG — AstraZeneca's own commentary validates FcRn mechanism preference. Soliris at -20% and Ultomiris growth slowing to +8% confirm the market is moving away from complement. This is a strategic crisis for Zilbrysq.

  3. UCB's dual-mechanism strategy (FcRn + C5) was designed for a market where both mechanisms had viable positioning — but the market has clearly chosen FcRn as preferred. UCB should consider whether its gMG resources are better concentrated on Rystiggo alone.

  4. The gMG market is expanding rapidly ($5.9B in 2025 and growing) — but Vyvgart is capturing disproportionate share (~70% of biologic revenue). UCB's combined ~5% market share means there's room to grow, but the competitive environment is getting harder, not easier, as Uplizna, Imaavy, and cemdisiran all enter the market.

5. Thesis Implications

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. Vyvgart moat is structural STRONGLY CONFIRMED $4.15B revenue, 90% growth, PFS adoption, seroneg. expansion imminent. Phase 2 prescription share increased to 69%.
2. Zilbrysq daily dosing terminal STRONGLY CONFIRMED Cemdisiran filed. 1 mention / 0 Rx in Phase 2. No improvement trajectory visible.
3. Mechanism-choice partnerships WEAKENED Phase 2 data shows no evidence HCPs think in terms of "UCB portfolio." They choose individual brands.
4. Uplizna dark horse SIGNAL CONFIRMED 3 prescriptions in 18 conversations, immediately post-launch. Fastest organic uptake of any non-Vyvgart brand.
5. Vyvgart switching triggers READY FOR ANALYSIS 40 conversations complete. Final report framing should lead with switching patterns.

6. What to Watch in Q2 2026

  1. ZoomRx Final Report delivery — expected May 2026. Vinoth should guide framing toward switching triggers and mechanism-class decision patterns, not just awareness metrics.
  2. Vyvgart seronegative PDUFA — May 10, 2026 — if approved, Vyvgart covers every MG subtype. This would be a massive competitive moment.
  3. UCB Q1 2026 earnings — will the Rystiggo+Zilbrysq trajectory show acceleration or deceleration vs. FY 2025 pace?
  4. Uplizna commercial traction — Q1 2026 will be the first full quarter of Uplizna availability. Watch for early prescription share data.
  5. Cemdisiran PDUFA date assignment — once Regeneron's filing is accepted, the FDA review clock starts. Expected approval late 2026 or early 2027.
  6. AAN 2026 data readouts (April) — Imaavy 2-year data, Vyvgart seroneg+CIDP updates, pipeline candidate presentations
  7. UCB R&D strategy — any signal on longer-acting Zilbrysq formulation or Rystiggo bolus injection development?

This multi-indication strategy creates the same flywheel that Epidiolex has in epilepsy — broader prescriber familiarity, institutional workflows, and formulary positioning that benefit the core gMG franchise.


Pre-filled syringe (Hytrulo) adoption accelerating: The pre-filled syringe enables 20-30 second self-injection — dramatically simpler than Rystiggo's infusion pump (15-30 minutes) or IV administration. This convenience gap is the single most important competitive dimension for patient preference.


Seronegative sBLA Priority Review (PDUFA May 10, 2026): If approved, Vyvgart covers every MG subtype — AChR+, MuSK+, LRP4+, and seronegative. This would give Vyvgart the broadest label in gMG, covering patient segments where UCB has no approved option.

As a C5 complement inhibitor, Zilbrysq is positioned in a mechanism class that the market is actively deprioritizing. Soliris biosimilars at 30% discounts create a low-cost complement option that makes Zilbrysq's premium pricing harder to justify. Cemdisiran's quarterly SC dosing (FDA filing in Q1 2026) will further compress Zilbrysq's competitive space. UCB's strategic decision: invest in Zilbrysq defense or redirect resources to Rystiggo's growth.
Synthesis: What Q1 2026 Competitor Earnings Tell UCB gMG


argenx's Vyvgart ($700M quarterly, +50%, $2.8B run rate) is building scale advantages that compound — multi-indication expansion, pre-filled syringe convenience, and seronegative label expansion create a product with the broadest coverage, simplest administration, and deepest prescriber familiarity in gMG. UCB's Rystiggo competes on niche differentiation, not market share.


The complement class is in structural decline in gMG — AstraZeneca's own commentary validates FcRn mechanism preference. Soliris at -20% and Ultomiris growth slowing to +8% confirm the market is moving away from complement. This is a strategic crisis for Zilbrysq.


UCB's dual-mechanism strategy (FcRn + C5) was designed for a market where both mechanisms had viable positioning — but the market has clearly chosen FcRn as preferred. UCB should consider whether its gMG resources are better concentrated on Rystiggo alone.


The gMG market is expanding rapidly ($5.9B in 2025 and growing) — but Vyvgart is capturing disproportionate share (~70% of biologic revenue). UCB's combined ~5% market share means there's room to grow, but the competitive environment is getting harder, not easier, as Uplizna, Imaavy, and cemdisiran all enter the market.
